zotero.abstractNote: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) often rely on the estimation of covariance matrices of electroencephalographic (EEG) signals. These matrices are symmetric positive definite (SPD) and lie on a Riemannian manifold. However, in many BCI applications, such as the analysis of cross-spectral density matrices, the matrices are Hermitian positive definite (HPD). In this paper, we extend the Riemannian framework for SPD matrices to the Hermitian manifold. We propose a novel feature extraction method based on the geometry of the HPD manifold and demonstrate its effectiveness on a motor imagery BCI dataset.
